LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT SPARK CONSTRUCTION, INC. DEMURRER TO RICHARD BOGAN'S CROSS-COMPLAINT, AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST PLEAD THE FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THE PROSECUTION OF THE CLRA ACTION WAS NOT IN GOOD FAITH. THE COURT'S RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT SPARK CONSTRUCTION, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. OBJECTIONS TO INTERROGATORIES NOT EVIDENCE THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT CANNOT PROVE HIS CASE. FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT SUFFICIENT TO MAINTAIN BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD TO PLEAD SPECIFIC FACTS REGARDING THE MISREPRESENTATIONS, WHAT THEY WERE, WHEN, WHERE, AND BY WHAT MEANS MADE (WRITTEN OR ORAL?). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT RICHARD BOGAN's Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections With Respect To Discovery Propounded By Plaintiff is moot in part and granted in part. The motion is moot as to the Requests For Admissions, Request For Production Of Documents, and Form Interrogatories as the order of February 16, 2010 rules upon those objections. The motion is granted as relief is granted as to the Special Interrogatories as to objections based upon attorney-client and attorney work product privileges, and mediation privilege. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

RULING:
Plaintiff's motion for protective was argued. The Court granted the motion and limited defendant Bogan to have 10 discoveries, 10 demands and 15 requests for admission. Prevailing party is to prepare an order for court's signature. Request for sanctions is denied. Judge: CURTIS KARNOW, Reporter: Sheila Chase of SHEILA CHASE & ASSOCIATES, CSR #4934, (415-333-7474)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT SPARK CONSTRUCTION, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: Craig Wallace, Esq. of McLennon Law Corporation, (415) 394-6688 appearing for Plaintiff/Cross Defendant Spark Construction, Inc. and moving party; Erin Palus, Esq. of Hooshmand Law Firm, (415) 835-5900 appearing for Defendant Richard Bogan, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to first cause of action for breach of contract. Objections to interrogatories not evidence that cross-complainant cannot prove his case. First Amended cross-complaint sufficient to maintain breach of contract cause of action. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend second cause of action for fraud to plead specific facts regarding the misrepresentations, what they were, when, where, and by what means made (written or oral?). Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Demurrer To Richard Bogan'S Cross-Complaint, And Request For Attorney'S Fees Under Civ.C. 1780(E); Request For Judicial Notice Supporting Spark Construction, Inc.'S Demurrer
Off Calendar Aug-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT SPARK CONSTRUCTION, INC. Demurrer to Richard Bogan's Cross-Complaint, and Request for Attorney's Fees. Appearances: Craig Wallace, Esq. of McLennon Law Corporation, (415) 394-6688 appearing for Plaintiff/Cross Defendant and moving party; John Roach, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 835-5900 appearing for Defendant/Cross-complainant, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. Cross-complainant must plead the fraud with specificity. Request for attorney fees denied. Cross-defendant fails to establish the prosecution of the CLRA action was not in good faith. The Court's ruling is without prejudice. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
